content: I'd like a few more reviews for <https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20230828104807.1581592-5-aliceryhl@google.com/>
FYI I am planning to put the patch set to use in the nvme driver within the next two weeks.

Andreas Hindborg said:

FYI I am planning to put the patch set to use in the nvme driver within the next two weeks.

On a related topic, @Mitchell Levy has started to use it for a Hyper-V util shutdown service POC based on some previous work from Wedson.

I'm actually surprised how fast Mitchell grasped the idea of workqueue and pin-init API (he only started to work on Linux kernel and Rust recently).
I am also planning to use the path in an async runtime for the kernel, but I can do also when it is merged
I give a first look a couple of weeks a go and they looks sounds, but I was planning to do one more round on them, maybe after the Summit

Alice Ryhl said:

I'd like a few more reviews for <https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20230828104807.1581592-5-aliceryhl@google.com/>

I've got a handful of patch series, general device and driver bindings are in it. In one of them there's container_of which makes use of offset_of, so I've sent my Reviewed-by to your series.

Martin Rodriguez Reboredo said:

Alice Ryhl said:

I'd like a few more reviews for <https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20230828104807.1581592-5-aliceryhl@google.com/>

I've got a handful of patch series, general device and driver bindings are in it. In one of them there's container_of which makes use of offset_of, so I've sent my Reviewed-by to your series.

Perhaps we should sync. I rebased a few patches related to PCI, device, io_mem, driver, revocations, etc. on top of rust-next https://github.com/metaspace/linux/commits/nvme for the nvme driver.

In the patch 0, Why/how need allocation when no support embedding, not need allocation when
support embedding? allocation which type?
@baul Sorry, I'm not completely sure what you mean.

baul said:

In the patch 0, Why/how need allocation when no support embedding, not need allocation when
support embedding? allocation which type?

If you don't have a work_struct embedded in the related struct, when you try to queue something in the workqeueue, you will need to allocate a work_struct (and maybe more) to be able to link the object in a workqueue linked list.

I just understand the key point: we are library authors. you are library user.
One of the primary goals behind the design used in this patch is that we(the rust workqueue library)
must support embedding the work_struct as a field in user-provided
types, because this allows you( the lib user) to submit things to the workqueue without
having to allocate (by yourself manually ), making the submission infallible. If we didn't have
to support this, then the patch would be much simpler. One of the main
things that make it complicated is that we must ensure that the function
pointer in the work_struct is compatible with the struct it is
contained within.
Note: things(allocate a work_struct (and maybe more)) always need be allocated, just impl in the lib, not by user. The origin stmt look like confused to me

[PATCH v4 2/7] rust: workqueue: add low-level workqueue bindings
a workqueue words should be the current workqueue or "the queue" in the Queue impl block.
I think in the internal of the pub struct Queue(Opaque<bindings::workqueue_struct>);, we should use the "the" or "current". For example, when do comment on the Queue:enqueue method, we should say

This may fail if the work item is already enqueued in the/current workqueue.


SAFETY: We only return `false` if the `work_struct` is already in the/current workqueue.

If not, someone such as me will think the Queue instance 's method Queue:enqueue can return any workqueue. Once i look at the kernel api of workqueue, it is not.

Should i send a mail to comment or just here?
No, I don't think saying "the" or "current" here makes sense. You can try to push the same work item to several different workqueues, but this will fail because it can only be in one at the itme.

we talk "the" in the rust Queue which have wrapped the specified c workqueue. So in this context, we only speak about the current Queue .
If we talk the enqueue outside of the rust Queue about a workitem, we can say a queue. .For example, if we say about the work item or rawworkitem, we can say a workqueue, such as your comment about/in RawWorkItem trait.

push the same work item to several different workqueues,

This is only possible when on different Queue, not the current Queue. Only the workitem(RawWorktime have a capabity to change workqueue, not the Queue.

I don't really understand what you're trying to say. The sentence "This may fail if the work item is already enqueued in a workqueue." is correct as written. The user might have previously pushed the work item to the same workqueue, or to a different workqueue. If that happens, then trying to push it again will fail, because it can only be in one queue at the time.

Ok, just understand your explanation. The C queue_work_on failure when 1. if the work item is already enqueued in the current queue 2. if the work item is already enqueued in a different workqueue. Thanks.
